channel.title: POLITICO Influence
channel.description: Delivered daily, Influence gives you a comprehensive rundown and analysis of all lobby hires and news on K Street.

item.title: A new player in clean energy advocacy
item.description: There’s a new player in the clean energy advocacy space — spurred by the policy failures of the industry.
item.dc:creator: By Debra Kahn, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Debra Kahn, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Prediction markets group makes first outside hire
item.description: The Coalition for Prediction Markets has hired a team of Democrats from Invariant as its first outside lobbyists amid growing congressional scrutiny over potential insider trading and intensifying regulatory disputes.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Hospitals’ clout in Washington is waning
item.description: Two of the most powerful lobbies in the country are turning on a third in the hopes of deflecting Washington’s wrath and securing bigger shares of the $5 trillion Americans spend on health care each year.
item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Chu and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Law firms continue fight against Trump EOs
item.description: President Donald Trump faces a tough battle to restore his executive orders targeting four major law firms following Thursday's arguments before a largely skeptical panel of three federal appellate judges.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Layoffs hit Penta Group
item.description: One of Washington’s largest public affairs consulting firms Penta Group laid off 37 staffers in the U.S. and Europe Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: The junk food lobby is winning on Capitol Hill
item.description: The “Make America Healthy Again” assault on junk food isn’t catching on in Congress, even as President Donald Trump and his supporters use the populist health movement to take shots at the deep-pocketed industry.
item.dc:creator: By Jennifer Scholtes and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jennifer Scholtes and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Ex-Dem fundraiser seeks a Trump pardon
item.description: Once-prolific Democratic fundraiser Hassan Nemazee has hired Mercury Public Affairs to lobby for a presidential pardon, according to a disclosure report filed Friday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Former Moroccan ambassador seeks pardon
item.description: A former Moroccan ambassador to the United Nations who was indicted in 2019 on visa fraud charges plans to seek a presidential pardon, according to a FARA registration filed with the Justice Department Thursday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Farragut lands a White House hire
item.description: Richard Chalkey is leaving the White House’s Office of Legislative Affairs to join Farragut Partners as a partner, the latest staffer to ditch Pennsylvania Ave for K Street.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Poll suggests businesses should engage Trump on policy more
item.description: Speaking out against the Trump administration on policy differences may not be as risky as corporate leaders think, according to a poll released Wednesday from the Public Affairs Council and Morning Consult.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Wall Street is losing its crypto war
item.description: Wall Street lobbyists are heading toward a stunning public defeat in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: A new class of super PACs is emerging. Voters are wary.
item.description: Pluralities of Americans say investing in cryptocurrency isn’t worth the risk and AI is developing too quickly, according to new results from The POLITICO Poll, even as those industries are spending millions to back industry-friendly candidates in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: FARA enforcement isn't dead yet
item.description: Florida Republican lobbyist and former member of Congress David Rivera was convicted today on charges of money laundering and failing to register as a foreign agent of Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: The latest White House departures
item.description: Office of Management and Budget aide Cameron Humphrey is leaving the Trump administration to join autonomous shipbuilder Saronic Technologies as a director of government relations.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Miyares’ next gig
item.description: Former Virginia Attorney General Jason Miyares is now honorary chair of the American Free Enterprise Chamber of Commerce, joining a leadership structure that includes former U.S. Attorney General Bill Barr and former Iowa governor and U.S. ambassador to China Terry Branstad.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: K Street reckons with prediction markets boom
item.description: It’s not just Congress: Think tanks and lobbying firms are also grappling with how to respond to the boom in prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket as they come to terms with the fact that their staffers often have access to a bevy of non-public information.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Holland & Knight loses six to Greenberg Traurig
item.description: Greenberg Traurig has poached a team of six lobbyists from Holland & Knight’s Washington office, including the co-leader of the Florida-based firm’s federal government affairs group.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Roger Stone is back
item.description: Roger Stone is back in the lobbying business. And he’s earning millions of dollars for his services.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Ex-Ukraine diplomat draws scrutiny over lithium stocks amid House run
item.description: One of Michigan Democratic congressional candidate Bridget Brink’s primary opponents is blasting her for having owned shares in a major American lithium company at the time she was the U.S. ambassador to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Lippman, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Lippman, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Health care industry’s big Q1
item.description: The biggest players in the health care industry are pouring record sums into K Street as the Trump administration intensifies scrutiny of their business practices ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Chu, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Calley Means blasts K St; Ballard remains king in Q1
item.description: Senior White House adviser Calley Means took a swipe at what he described as a vast apparatus in Washington “built to make change very hard.” Plus, dive into our quarterly LDA rankings.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: New moves for Paul Ryan, Annie Kuster
item.description: Former Speaker Paul Ryan is joining the strategic advisory board for defense tech startup EdgeRunner AI.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Venezuela’s Rodríguez makes first FARA hire
item.description: Acting Venezuelan President Delcy Rodríguez has hired an employment lawyer from California to represent her in legal matters, according to a FARA registration filed earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Transpo donors boost Duffy’s son-in-law
item.description: Wisconsin congressional candidate Michael Alfonso’s campaign raked in donations from dozens of lobbyists and industry executives in the first quarter of the year, and many of them seem to have one thing in common: they stand to profit from currying favor with Alfonso’s father-in-law, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Dems ditch Swalwell’s AI startup
item.description: Congressional Democrats are cutting ties with Eric Swalwell’s AI startup in the wake of sexual assault allegations. More than half a dozen Democratic campaigns that previously used Findraiser AI’s services told PI they’re no longer working with the company.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: CPAC lands in new Hungarian PM’s crosshairs
item.description: On the heels of his landslide victory over Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, opposition leader Péter Magyar has set his sights on a target for his anti-corruption efforts: the Conservative Political Action Conference.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Vance world’s growing K Street presence
item.description: Former JD Vance aides are slowly but surely filling the ranks of K Street firms.
item.dc:creator: By Alex Gangitano, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Alex Gangitano, Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Inside AEI’s housing bill opposition
item.description: The American Enterprise Institute's opposition to limiting large investors from acquiring single-family homes goes deeper than ideological objections: its leadership has deep ties to the institutional investors that stand to profit from the status quo.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler, Daniel Lippman and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler, Daniel Lippman and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Lobbyist found liable for PPP fraud
item.description: Lobbyist Vicki Iseman has been ordered to pay the government more than $400,000 after a judge found she fraudulently obtained Paycheck Protection Program loans for her lobbying business during the Covid-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: A pharma legend bows out
item.description: Lobbying legend Stephen Ubl will step down as chief executive of the Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America at the end of the year after leading the drugmaker trade group for a decade, PhRMA announced today.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
